commonProblems.h2: Toyota GT86 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Toyota GT86's FA20 boxer engine is generally robust but suffers from three well-documented issues: excessive oil consumption on early models, timing chain tensioner failures, and valve spring recalls on 2012-2013 units. Every rebuilt FA20 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded piston rings, revised tensioners and recall-compliant valve springs - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - Why Early Toyota GT86 FA20 Engines Burn Oil
problems.rootCause: Factory piston rings allow excessive oil passage into combustion chamber; worsened by short-trip driving and infrequent oil changes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Regular oil top-ups, switch to higher-viscosity synthetic, PCV valve replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary mitigation only; does not fix root cause

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine removal, cylinder head off, new piston rings fitted, reassembly
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; 40k-60k mile solution if block not scored

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit upgraded rebuilt FA20 with forged rings, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; upgraded components prevent recurrence, 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: On a GT86 worth £8,000-£15,000, a rebuilt FA20 with upgraded rings offers better long-term value than an in-situ ring job - especially if the block shows scoring. We recommend replacement if oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FA20 engine prices for your Toyota GT86

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Cold Start Rattle on Toyota GT86
problems.affectedModels: Toyota GT86 GT, Pro, Club Series (2012-2021, FA20D-4U & FA20D-4V)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time; chain slap on cold start accelerates guide wear and can cause catastrophic jump.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front cover removal, tensioner swap, timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Short-term fix; guides may already be worn

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain, tensioner, guides, sprockets replaced; engine timed precisely
Supply & fit rebuilt FA20 with upgraded timing components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if chain has jumped or valves contacted; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If you hear cold-start rattle but no codes, fit a full timing kit preventatively. If the chain has jumped or you have misfires, replacement is safer and often cheaper than repairing valve damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for FA20 timing chain replacement Toyota GT86

problems.h4: Valve Spring Recall - 2012-2013 Toyota GT86 Safety Update
problems.affectedModels: Toyota GT86 GT, Pro (2012-2013, FA20D-4U, VINs before ZN6-xxxxx update)
problems.rootCause: Sub-spec valve springs can fracture under high RPM, causing valve drop and catastrophic engine damage; Toyota issued recall T-SB-0025-18.

repairOptions.tier: Recall Fix - Valve Spring Replacement Only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head removal, spring replacement, valve inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if springs fractured before valve damage occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, valve guide/seal replacement, spring upgrade, resurface
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses damage but leaves original block/pistons

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit recall-compliant rebuilt FA20 with upgraded springs throughout
repairOptions.longevity: Only guaranteed solution if valve drop occurred; includes warranty

problems.recommendation: If your GT86 is within the recall VIN range and hasn't had the update, get it done immediately - free at Toyota. If damage has occurred, a rebuilt engine is more reliable and cost-effective than head-only repair.
problems.cta: Check if your Toyota GT86 qualifies for valve spring recall fix

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Noise - FA20 "Cricket" Sound Fix
problems.affectedModels: Toyota GT86 GT, Pro, TRD (2017-2021, FA20D-4V)
problems.rootCause: Denso HPFP internal wear creates metallic "cricket" chirp; can lead to fuel pressure loss, lean codes and potential piston damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Pump removal, new Denso unit fitted, fuel system primed
repairOptions.longevity: Fixes noise; use OEM-spec pump to avoid recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt FA20 with inspected fuel system, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Only needed if lean condition caused piston/cylinder damage

problems.recommendation: HPFP noise alone doesn't require engine replacement - swap the pump early. Only consider full replacement if diagnostic codes confirm lean misfire or compression loss.
problems.cta: Compare FA20D-4V engine prices for Toyota GT86 facelift models

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Toyota GT86
finalCta.paragraph: The FA20 engine's known issues are well understood and fixable. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who supply rebuilt FA20 units with upgraded piston rings, revised tensioners and recall-compliant components - all backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices from vetted Toyota GT86 engine specialists and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Toyota GT86 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Toyota GT86 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Toyota GT86 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Toyota GT86's production timeline (2012-2021), organised so owners can quickly identify their FA20 engine code and compare rebuilt replacement prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: GT86 Launch & Valve Spring Recall Years
years.description: The GT86 launched with the FA20D-4U engine; early VINs required a valve spring recall update to prevent catastrophic failure.
keyChanges: UK launch of Toyota GT86 with FA20D-4U 2.0L boxer petrol
Valve spring recall issued for VINs before mid-2013 (T-SB-0025-18)
Manual (TL70) and automatic (A960E) transmission options available
knownFor: Valve spring fracture risk on early VINs (recall critical)
Oil consumption complaints begin to emerge in owner forums
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2013 Toyota GT86 replacement engines
years.ticker: Toyota GT86 valve spring recall fix • FA20D-4U oil consumption repair • GT86 2012 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Toyota GT86 valve spring recall fix

years.description: Mid-production FA20D-4U units saw incremental improvements but oil consumption remained the dominant owner concern.
keyChanges: Valve spring recall completed on affected early VINs
Minor PCV and gasket updates to address oil burn complaints
knownFor: Oil consumption (1L/1,000 miles) common on high-mileage examples
Timing chain tensioner rattle reported on cold starts
Strong enthusiast community and aftermarket support
years.ticker: GT86 FA20 oil burn fix • 2015 GT86 engine replacement • FA20D-4U piston ring upgrade

years.description: The 2017 facelift introduced the FA20D-4V code with revised PCV, updated coil packs and improved oil control.
keyChanges: FA20D-4V engine code introduced with revised PCV system
Updated ignition coils and HPFP to address "cricket" noise
TRD Edition and Monte Carlo special variants launched
mainEngines: FA20D-4V - 2.0L petrol (GT, Pro, TRD, Monte Carlo)
knownFor: Reduced oil consumption vs early FA20D-4U units
HPFP "cricket" chirp remains a known characteristic
Strong residual values and enthusiast demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Toyota GT86 replacement engines
years.ticker: GT86 FA20D-4V HPFP noise fix • 2018 GT86 engine replacement • Facelift FA20 rebuild cost

years.description: The final GT86 model years featured the Club Series limited edition and the most refined FA20D-4V specification before GR86 replacement.
keyChanges: Club Series limited edition launched (2020) with unique trim
Final production year 2021; GR86 (FA24) announced as successor
No further mechanical changes to FA20D-4V specification
mainEngines: FA20D-4V - 2.0L petrol (GT, Pro, Club Series, Final Edition)
knownFor: Most reliable FA20 specification (lowest oil consumption)
High collector interest in Club Series and Final Edition
Strong demand for low-mileage replacement FA20 units
years.ticker: GT86 Club Series engine replacement • 2021 FA20D-4V rebuild price • Final edition GT86 engine availability

faq.h2: Toyota GT86 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Toyota GT86 FA20 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Toyota GT86 FA20 engine typically costs £2,800-£5,400 supply-only in the UK, depending on variant, engine code (FA20D-4U vs FA20D-4V) and whether upgraded components are included.
keyPoints: Early FA20D-4U units (2012-2016) start from £2,800 rebuilt
Facelift FA20D-4V units (2017-2021) range £3,200-£5,400
Supply & fit adds £800-£1,500 labour depending on location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt FA20 engine prices for your Toyota GT86

items.question: Does the Toyota GT86 engine replacement fix oil consumption problems?
items.answer: Yes - rebuilt FA20 engines supplied through EngineMarket include upgraded piston rings and revised PCV components specifically designed to address the GT86's known oil consumption issue.
keyPoints: Early FA20D-4U engines suffered from factory ring design
Rebuilt units use forged or upgraded ring sets
Revised PCV and baffle plates improve oil control
Expect normal consumption (<0.5L/5,000 miles) post-rebuild
items.cta: Get quotes for oil-consumption-fixed Toyota GT86 engines

items.question: Is the Toyota GT86 timing chain tensioner recall included in replacement engines?
items.answer: All rebuilt FA20 engines include updated tensioners and timing components; if your GT86 is within the 2012-2013 valve spring recall VIN range, we fit recall-compliant springs as standard.
keyPoints: FA20 rebuilt engines use revised tensioner designs
Valve spring recall (T-SB-0025-18) parts fitted where applicable
Timing kit replaced as part of standard rebuild process
Documentation provided for MOT and warranty validation
items.cta: Check recall-compliant FA20 engine availability for your GT86

items.question: What is the difference between FA20D-4U and FA20D-4V Toyota GT86 engines?
items.answer: FA20D-4U (2012-2016) is the early GT86 engine code with higher oil consumption risk; FA20D-4V (2017-2021) is the facelift version with revised PCV, updated coils and improved oil control.
keyPoints: Both are 2.0L naturally aspirated boxer petrol, 197hp
FA20D-4V has revised PCV and coil pack design
FA20D-4U more prone to oil burn and valve spring issues
Mechanically interchangeable with ECU sync if swapping codes
items.cta: Compare FA20D-4U vs FA20D-4V engine prices for GT86

items.question: Will a replacement FA20 engine fit my Toyota GT86 Pro trim?
items.answer: Yes - all FA20 engine codes (FA20D-4U, FA20D-4V, 4U-GSE) are physically compatible with every UK GT86 variant including GT, Pro, Club Series and TRD Edition.
keyPoints: FA20 block, mounts and ancillaries are identical across trims
ECU may require coding if swapping early/late engine codes
Transmission compatibility (manual/auto) must be matched
DVLA registration check confirms exact fitment requirements
items.cta: Verify FA20 compatibility for your Toyota GT86 variant

items.question: Can I use a Subaru BRZ engine in my Toyota GT86?
items.answer: Yes - the Subaru BRZ uses the same FA20 engine family, but ECU immobiliser and wiring harness differences require specialist syncing for plug-and-play fitment in a GT86.
keyPoints: FA20 block and internals are mechanically identical
BRZ ECU/immobiliser must be synced or replaced
Ancillary brackets and mounts may differ slightly
EngineMarket specialists handle cross-brand compatibility
items.cta: Enquire about BRZ-sourced FA20 engine compatibility for GT86

items.question: How long does Toyota GT86 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only FA20 engines dispatch in 2-5 working days; supply & fit installations typically take 2-3 working days at a vetted UK workshop, depending on parts availability and workshop schedule.
keyPoints: Rebuilt FA20 units are bench-tested before dispatch
Most UK specialists hold FA20 cores for quick turnaround
Complex jobs (ECU sync, recall updates) may add 1 day
Nationwide delivery available; collection options for core return
items.cta: Get a timeline quote for your Toyota GT86 engine replacement

items.question: Does Toyota GT86 engine replacement include a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every FA20 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from the supplying specialist; extended coverage options are available at checkout.
keyPoints: Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Unlimited mileage terms apply to rebuilt and remanufactured units
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month minimum coverage
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before booking
items.cta: Compare warranty terms on Toyota GT86 rebuilt engines

items.question: What oil should I use after Toyota GT86 engine replacement?
items.answer: Toyota specifies 0W-20 fully synthetic for the FA20 engine; after a rebuild, follow the specialist's break-in procedure (typically 500-1,000 miles) before switching to long-life synthetic.
keyPoints: Use Toyota-approved 0W-20 or equivalent ACEA C2/C3 synthetic
Break-in oil may be specified for first 500 miles post-install
Change oil and filter at 500 miles, then every 10k miles/12 months
Avoid short-trip driving during break-in to seat rings properly
items.cta: Get post-install maintenance guidance for your rebuilt FA20

items.question: How do I know which Toyota GT86 engine code I need?
items.answer: Enter your registration number on EngineMarket to instantly identify your GT86's exact engine code (FA20D-4U, FA20D-4V or 4U-GSE), variant and compatible replacement options via DVLA data.
keyPoints: Engine code is stamped on the block near the oil filter housing
DVLA lookup confirms chassis, variant and emission standard
Our compatibility checker cross-references trim, year and transmission
items.cta: Identify your Toyota GT86 engine code free via registration check
